task_completion.description: Create an integer slider n ranging from 1 to 20
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the GeoGebra Algebra view or Graphics view, there should be a slider named 'n'. The Algebra view should show it as an integer (e.g., n = 5) and its settings or behavior should indicate a range up to 20.

task_completion.description: Create the Fourier partial sum function using the Sum command
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra view must show a function (e.g., g(x)) defined using the 'Sum' command, matching the formula (4/π) * Sum(sin((2k-1)x)/(2k-1), k, 1, n). A corresponding wavy curve should be visible in the Graphics view.

task_completion.description: Create the target square wave function with period 2π and amplitude 1
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should show a function defining a square wave (e.g., using sgn(sin(x)) or an If statement). The Graphics view should display a square wave alternating between y=1 and y=-1, crossing the x-axis at multiples of π.
The square wave and the Fourier approximation curve must have visibly different styling in the Graphics view, such as different colors, line thicknesses, or line styles (e.g., one solid, one dashed).

task_completion.description: Include a text annotation displaying the value of n
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text object must be visible in the Graphics view that dynamically displays the current value of the slider n (e.g., 'n = 5' or similar text).

task_completion.description: Save the project as fourier_square_wave.ggb in the correct directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GeoGebra window title should display 'fourier_square_wave.ggb', or a save dialog/terminal window should show the file being saved to '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/fourier_square_wave.ggb'.

integrity.description: Agent used the GeoGebra GUI to construct the applet
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the GeoGebra application interface (Algebra view, Graphics view, input bar) being actively used, rather than a text editor writing a raw XML/.ggb file.

integrity.description: Agent used a dynamic Sum command rather than hardcoding a specific partial sum
integrity.visual_evidence: The Algebra view should explicitly show the 'Sum' command linked to the variable 'n', rather than a long, hardcoded string of sine additions (e.g., sin(x) + sin(3x)/3 + ...).

integrity.description: Agent saved the file through genuine interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: The file save process should be visible via GeoGebra's 'Save' or 'Export' dialogs, or verified via terminal commands showing the file exists in the target directory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Fourier partial sum MUST be implemented using the GeoGebra 'Sum' command with the formula (4/π) * Sum(sin((2k-1)x)/(2k-1), k, 1, n). The target square wave has a period of 2π and amplitude of 1, which can be constructed using 'sgn(sin(x))' or 'If(sin(x)>=0, 1, -1)'. The slider 'n' must have a range of 1 to 20. The file must be saved exactly as 'fourier_square_wave.ggb' in '~/Documents/GeoGebra/projects/'. Note: The 'Sum' command is a strict requirement; if it is missing from the Algebra view, the agent has failed the core mathematical component of the task.
